title: RDS Encryption at Rest — CMK policy, backup encryption, parameter-group defaults
description: An engineer is provisioning an RDS instance (or Aurora cluster) for a production workload. Target end state: storage encryption with a customer-managed KMS CMK, backup encryption using the same CMK, TLS-in-transit enforced at the parameter group, IAM database authentication enabled for appropriate engines, deletion protection on, and logs (general / error / slow / audit depending on engine) exported to CloudWatch Logs encrypted with a separate CMK.
actors: Application workload (semi-trusted — acts on behalf of end users)
assets: Database logs (may contain PII in query strings)

stride.name: Unencrypted storage disclosure
stride.description: An RDS instance created without storage encryption leaves the underlying EBS plaintext; a snapshot sharing attack (or account compromise) reveals all data. Hulumi components (shipping v0.3+) default to SSE-KMS with the `AccountFoundation` data-key CMK.

stride.name: Unencrypted backup disclosure
stride.description: Even with encrypted primary storage, automated/manual snapshots may be created without encryption if the CMK policy excludes the backup service principal. Mitigation: KMS key policy explicitly grants `kms:CreateGrant` to `backup.rds.amazonaws.com` and deny any non-encrypted snapshot creation via SCP.

stride.name: Plaintext TLS-in-transit bypass
stride.description: Without parameter-group-enforced TLS, application clients may connect over plaintext. Mitigation: parameter group sets `rds.force_ssl=1` (PostgreSQL) / equivalent per engine; Hulumi baseline applies the engine-appropriate flag.

stride.name: Overly broad DB authentication
stride.description: Without IAM database authentication (where supported: MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB), credentials live in Secrets Manager / app code. IAM auth + Secrets Manager rotation is the hardened path; `AccountFoundation.secretsManager` namespace supports rotation hooks.

stride.name: Missing audit logs
stride.description: RDS audit logs (PostgreSQL `pgaudit` / MySQL general log / Aurora audit) are off by default. Without them, SQL-level attribution is impossible. Hulumi Startup-Hardened tier flips audit on and exports to CloudWatch Logs with CMK encryption.

stride.name: Accidental drop via IaC bug
stride.description: Deletion protection off + a broken `pulumi refresh` could propose a destroy for an RDS instance. Mitigation: deletion protection on in both tiers; Hulumi `DriftClassifier` (v0.4+) flags the provider-plan diff before apply.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.baseline.aws.SecureRds
recommendedComponents.rationale: Planned ComponentResource covering storage encryption, backup CMK, TLS parameter group, IAM auth, deletion protection. For v1 users, compose raw `aws.rds.Instance` with the KMS keys from `AccountFoundation.kmsRing` as an interim pattern.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.baseline.aws.AccountFoundation.kmsRing
recommendedComponents.rationale: Per-service CMKs (`data`, `logs`, `secrets`, `config`). The RDS instance consumes the `data` key; its CloudWatch logs consume the `logs` key.

recommendedComponents.name: hulumi.policies.aws.CisV5Pack
recommendedComponents.rationale: Includes CIS v5.0.0 §2.3 (RDS encryption at rest) as a preventive CrossGuard check, complementing Security Hub's detective finding.
